# API Workaround Checklist: Bulk Operations

**Purpose**: Validate that the spec and plan accurately document OmniJS bulk API behavior, return types, error semantics, position construction patterns, and the "section" concept -- ensuring requirements are grounded in actual API contracts rather than assumptions
**Created**: 2026-03-18
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md) | [plan.md](../plan.md) | [data-model.md](../data-model.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## moveTasks() Return Value and Error Behavior

- [ ] CHK001 - Is the `moveTasks()` return type explicitly documented as void in the spec? [Completeness, Spec Clarifications Session 2026-03-18]
- [ ] CHK002 - Are requirements specified for how the server obtains post-move task properties given `moveTasks()` returns void (must re-read from original task objects)? [Completeness, Spec Assumptions]
- [ ] CHK003 - Is the behavior documented when `moveTasks()` is called with an array containing a single task (the one-at-a-time pattern) vs. multiple tasks? [Clarity, Plan AD-01]
- [ ] CHK004 - Are requirements defined for what happens when `moveTasks()` throws an OmniJS exception (e.g., moving a task into itself as a subtask)? [Coverage, Spec Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK005 - Is it specified that `moveTasks()` silently succeeds when moving a task to its current location (no-op behavior)? [Clarity, Spec Edge Cases]
- [x] CHK006 - Are requirements documented for how the server detects whether a move actually occurred, given `moveTasks()` returns void and does not throw on no-op? [Gap -- RESOLVED: Plan AD-12 documents post-move verification via property re-read pattern]
- [ ] CHK007 - Is the `moveTasks()` position parameter type (`Project, Task, or Task.ChildInsertionLocation`) accurately reflected in the TaskPosition schema? [Consistency, Spec Clarifications]

## duplicateTasks() Return Value and New Task Objects

- [ ] CHK008 - Is the `duplicateTasks()` return type (`TaskArray` of new copies in library order) explicitly documented in the spec? [Completeness, Spec Clarifications Session 2026-03-18]
- [ ] CHK009 - Are requirements specified for how the server correlates returned copies with input items, given the API returns copies in library order (not input order)? [Clarity, Spec Clarifications]
- [ ] CHK010 - Is the one-at-a-time calling pattern for `duplicateTasks()` documented as the solution to the library-order correlation problem? [Completeness, Plan AD-01]
- [ ] CHK011 - Are requirements defined for extracting `newId` and `newName` from the returned `TaskArray` object (accessing `id.primaryKey` and `name`)? [Completeness, Data Model - BulkBatchItemResult]
- [ ] CHK012 - Is it documented that duplicated tasks inherit all properties (name, note, tags, dates, flagged status, subtasks) from the original? [Completeness, Spec FR-002]
- [ ] CHK013 - Are requirements specified for the `markIncomplete()` call on duplicated tasks to reset completion status (FR-011)? [Completeness, Plan AD-06]
- [x] CHK014 - Is it specified whether `duplicateTasks()` returns a single-element `TaskArray` when called with a single-task array, and how to access element [0]? [Clarity, Gap -- RESOLVED: Data-model duplicate_tasks section documents single-element TaskArray access via [0]]

## convertTasksToProjects() Return Value and Subtask Preservation

- [ ] CHK015 - Is the `convertTasksToProjects()` return type (`Array of Project`) explicitly documented in the spec? [Completeness, Spec Clarifications Session 2026-03-18]
- [ ] CHK016 - Is the `convertTasksToProjects()` position parameter type (`Folder or Folder.ChildInsertionLocation`) accurately documented as different from the task operations position type? [Consistency, Spec Clarifications]
- [ ] CHK017 - Are requirements specified for how subtasks are preserved during conversion (do they become the project's child tasks automatically, or does the server need additional logic)? [Clarity, Spec FR-003]
- [ ] CHK018 - Is it documented that the original task is consumed (removed from its location) after conversion? [Completeness, Data Model State Transitions]
- [ ] CHK019 - Are requirements defined for which task properties transfer to the new project (name, note confirmed; tags and dates "where applicable" is ambiguous)? [Ambiguity, Spec Acceptance Scenario 4.2]
- [ ] CHK020 - Is the "where applicable" qualifier for tag and date transfer during conversion defined with specific rules for which properties map to project equivalents? [Clarity, Spec Acceptance Scenario 4.2]
- [ ] CHK021 - Are requirements specified for how `newId` and `newName` are extracted from the returned `Project` object? [Completeness, Data Model - BulkBatchItemResult]
- [ ] CHK022 - Is the behavior documented when converting a task that has no subtasks (should produce an empty project per Acceptance Scenario 4.4)? [Completeness, Spec Acceptance Scenario 4.4]
- [ ] CHK023 - Is it specified that `convertTasksToProjects()` accepts only `Folder or Folder.ChildInsertionLocation` for position, NOT `Project` or `Task`? [Consistency, Spec Clarifications]

## Position Object Construction Patterns

- [ ] CHK024 - Are all valid position construction patterns documented (`project.ending`, `project.beginning`, `folder.beginning`, `folder.ending`, `inbox.ending`, `inbox.beginning`, `task.ending`, `task.beginning`)? [Completeness, Data Model TaskPosition]
- [ ] CHK025 - Are the `before`/`after` position patterns documented (`siblingTask.before`, `siblingTask.after` for tasks; `siblingSection.before`, `siblingSection.after` for sections)? [Completeness, Data Model TaskPosition]
- [ ] CHK026 - Is it specified that `library.beginning` and `library.ending` are the position patterns for placing sections at the library root? [Completeness, Data Model SectionPosition]
- [ ] CHK027 - Is the distinction between `Task.ChildInsertionLocation` and `Folder.ChildInsertionLocation` documented as the reason for separate TaskPosition and SectionPosition schemas? [Clarity, Plan AD-03]
- [x] CHK028 - Is it documented that a bare `Project` or `Task` object can serve as a position parameter (equivalent to `project.ending` / `task.ending` respectively)? [Gap -- RESOLVED: Plan AD-11 explicitly prohibits bare objects; requires ChildInsertionLocation via bracket/dot notation]
- [ ] CHK029 - Are requirements specified for how the OmniJS script constructs position objects from the flat schema fields (e.g., resolving `projectId` + `placement: "beginning"` into `project.beginning`)? [Completeness, Quickstart - Key Patterns]
- [ ] CHK030 - Is the `relativeTo` field requirement for `before`/`after` placements documented with the OmniJS expression it maps to (`sibling.before` / `sibling.after`)? [Completeness, Data Model TaskPosition]
- [ ] CHK031 - Is it specified that the `placement` field defaults to `ending` for task operations but is required (no default) for section operations? [Clarity, Spec Key Entities - Position]

## Invalid Target / Self-Reference Error Behavior

- [ ] CHK032 - Are requirements defined for what OmniJS does when moving a task into itself as a subtask (throws exception vs. silent failure)? [Coverage, Spec Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK033 - Is the error handling strategy for self-referential moves documented (catch OmniJS exception per-item, return OPERATION_FAILED with native error message)? [Completeness, Spec Clarifications]
- [x] CHK034 - Are requirements defined for what happens when duplicating a task to itself as a position target? [Coverage, Gap -- RESOLVED: Spec edge case added distinguishing beginning/ending (circular, OPERATION_FAILED) vs before/after (valid sibling copy)]
- [ ] CHK035 - Is it specified that circular hierarchy detection for sections (folder moved into itself) relies on OmniJS internal validation, not server-side pre-validation? [Clarity, Spec Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK036 - Are requirements defined for what happens when the target project is completed or dropped (move succeeds with warning)? [Completeness, Spec Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK037 - Is the `warning` field in `BulkBatchItemResult` documented as a new field unique to bulk-tools (not present in existing batch schemas)? [Completeness, Spec Edge Cases]
- [x] CHK038 - Are requirements specified for how the server detects an inactive target to populate the `warning` field (checking `project.status` before moving)? [Gap -- RESOLVED: Plan AD-13 documents status check against Project.Status.Active/Done/Dropped/OnHold as pre-loop check]

## moveSections() vs moveTasks() -- "Section" Definition

- [ ] CHK039 - Is the term "section" explicitly defined as "a Project or a Folder" consistent with the OmniJS API signature (`sections: Array of Project or Folder`)? [Clarity, Spec Key Entities]
- [ ] CHK040 - Is the distinction between `moveSections()` position parameter (`Folder or Folder.ChildInsertionLocation`) and `moveTasks()` position parameter (`Project, Task, or Task.ChildInsertionLocation`) clearly documented? [Consistency, Spec Clarifications]
- [x] CHK041 - Are requirements defined for how the server resolves whether an item identifier refers to a folder or a project when processing `move_sections` / `duplicate_sections`? [Completeness, Gap -- RESOLVED: Plan AD-14 and data-model move_sections/duplicate_sections document Folder-then-Project probe pattern]
- [ ] CHK042 - Is it documented that `moveSections()` returns void (same as `moveTasks()`), requiring the server to re-read properties from the original objects after the move? [Completeness, Spec Clarifications]
- [ ] CHK043 - Is it documented that `duplicateSections()` returns `SectionArray` (copies in library order), matching the same correlation challenge as `duplicateTasks()`? [Completeness, Spec Clarifications]
- [ ] CHK044 - Are requirements defined for what happens when a mix of folders and projects are passed in a single `move_sections` / `duplicate_sections` batch? [Completeness, Spec Acceptance Scenario 5.3]
- [ ] CHK045 - Is the `SectionPosition` schema documented as reusing the existing `PositionSchema` from folder-tools, and is this reuse justified? [Consistency, Data Model SectionPosition]

## Requirement Consistency

- [ ] CHK046 - Is the spec consistent with the actual OmniJS API regarding `moveTasks()` accepting `inbox` as a position target (the API accepts `Task.ChildInsertionLocation` which includes `inbox.beginning`/`inbox.ending`)? [Consistency, Spec FR-001]
- [ ] CHK047 - Does the spec's claim that `moveSections()` accepts "only Folder or Folder.ChildInsertionLocation" match the actual API documentation? [Consistency, Spec Clarifications]
- [ ] CHK048 - Is the `convertTasksToProjects()` API example in the spec (`convertTasksToProjects(inbox, library.ending)`) consistent with the actual API signature and usage? [Consistency, Spec Assumptions]
- [ ] CHK049 - Are the `BulkBatchItemResult` fields (`newId`, `newName`) consistently documented across all tools that need them (duplicate_tasks, duplicate_sections, convert_tasks_to_projects) and omitted from tools that do not (move_tasks, move_sections, batch_update_tasks)? [Consistency, Data Model]
- [ ] CHK050 - Is the per-item error handling pattern (catch per-item in OmniJS loop, return OPERATION_FAILED) consistently specified across all 6 tools? [Consistency, Spec Clarifications]

## Edge Case Coverage

- [x] CHK051 - Are requirements defined for what happens when `moveTasks()` is called with an empty array after filtering out invalid items (zero valid items to move)? [Coverage, Gap -- RESOLVED: Spec edge case added: outer success:true with summary {succeeded:0, failed:N} per dropItems/markComplete pattern]
- [ ] CHK052 - Are requirements specified for what `duplicateTasks()` does with a task that has deeply nested subtask hierarchies (are all levels duplicated)? [Coverage, Spec FR-002]
- [x] CHK053 - Are requirements defined for the maximum subtask depth supported by `convertTasksToProjects()` for subtask preservation? [Coverage, Gap -- RESOLVED: Spec edge case added: no depth limit, OmniJS handles arbitrarily deep hierarchies natively]
- [x] CHK054 - Are requirements specified for what happens when the `relativeTo` task/section in a position has been deleted between validation and execution? [Coverage, Gap -- RESOLVED: Plan AD-15 documents single-IIFE model; relativeTo resolved once at script start, eliminating intra-batch race conditions. Spec edge case updated to top-level error scope]
- [ ] CHK055 - Is the `RELATIVE_TARGET_NOT_FOUND` error code documented with the specific OmniJS behavior that triggers it? [Completeness, Research RT-08]

## Notes

- Check items off as completed: `[x]`
- Add comments or findings inline
- Items are numbered sequentially for easy reference
- API documentation source: https://omni-automation.com/omnifocus/OF-API.html
